headline: Anthropic co-founder predicts intelligence explosion within 3 years.
summary: Jack Clark delivered the 2026 Cosmos Lecture at Oxford on May 20 — "Change is inevitable.

headline: Google I/O 2026: Gemini 3.5 Flash GA, Spark, DeepMind talent deal.
summary: At Google I/O on 19 May, Sundar Pichai opened with "We are firmly in our agentic Gemini era." Gemini 3.5 Flash shipped to general availability the same day as the default model in the Gemini app and Google Search AI Mode worldwide; Gemini 3.5 Pro was held back for June.

headline: Anthropic: Claude Managed Agents get dreaming, memory, outcomes — shipped at Code w/ Claude.
summary: On 6 May, at Anthropic's inaugural developer conference in San Francisco, the company shipped three features for its Managed Agents platform: (a) *Memory* (public beta) — filesystem-based cross-session learning with audit logs and portable stores, so agents carry context between runs; (b) *Dreaming*

headline: Anthropic: KPMG global alliance (276K employees) + Stainless acquisition.
summary: In a single week: KPMG on 19 May committed to deploy Claude across its entire 276,000-person global workforce — the largest known enterprise Claude rollout by headcount.

headline: OpenAI: Codex in mobile + personal finance preview.
summary: On 14–15 May, OpenAI expanded two distinct product surfaces.

headline: Mistral: Medium 3.5 + Remote Agents in Vibe (2 May).
summary: Mistral released Medium 3.5 on 2 May alongside a cloud coding-agent platform update.

headline: Mistral Medium 3.5
summary: (released 2 May 2026) — Dense 128B-parameter model.

headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash
summary: (GA: 19 May 2026) — Google's new Flash-tier flagship, succeeding Gemini 3.1 Flash.

headline: Kimi K2.6
summary: (Moonshot AI, ~April 2026) — Currently joint-top open-weight model on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at 54 points, tied with Xiaomi's MiMo V2.5 Pro, ahead of DeepSeek V4 Pro (52).

headline: DeepSeek V4 Pro / Flash
summary: (released 24 April 2026) — V4 Pro scores 52 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index.

headline: Zyphra ZAYA1-8B
summary: (released 6–7 May 2026) — 8B total parameters, ~760M active per token via MoE routing.

headline: SWE-Bench Verified (May 2026 standings).
summary: The 500-task GitHub-issue agentic benchmark (single attempt, pass@1, official verified set at swebench.com) currently shows: Mistral Medium 3.5 **77.6%** [vendor PR]; Devstral 2 **72.2%**; Claude Opus 4.7 **64.3%**; Gemini 3.5 Flash **55.1%**.

headline: Open-weights models close to within 6 points of closed frontier (Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index).
summary: The index aggregates coding, reasoning, instruction-following, and multimodal dimensions.

headline: LMArena user-preference leaderboard (May 2026).
summary: Claude Opus 4.6 (thinking) and Opus 4.7 (thinking) rank at the top of Text Generation Elo.

headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash agentic benchmarks: new frameworks warrant scrutiny.
summary: Google's I/O-reported numbers — GDPval-AA (1656 Elo) and MCP Atlas (83.6%) — are on benchmarks that are not yet widely peer-replicated.

headline: EU AI Act amended: high-risk deadline pushed to December 2027, nudifier AI banned.
summary: On 7 May, EU institutions reached provisional agreement on the Digital Omnibus on AI — the first amendment package since the Act's June 2024 adoption.

headline: OpenAI: $25B+ ARR, IPO groundwork, Hiro Finance acqui-hire.
summary: OpenAI has surpassed $25 billion in annualised revenue, and CFO Sarah Friar confirmed plans to reserve retail shares in a potential public listing the company is targeting for H2 2026 [single-source on retail detail].

headline: Anthropic fundraising and chip discussions.
summary: Reports indicate Anthropic is closing a ~$5B round at a $170B valuation [single-source; independent confirmation not found].

headline: Shield AI closes $1.5B Series G at $12.7B valuation.
summary: Defense-tech AI company Shield AI — developer of autonomous military aviation systems including the Heron fighter pilot AI — closed a $1.5B Series G as part of a broader $2.25B capital package.

headline: US AI safety evaluation program extended to DeepMind, Microsoft, xAI.
summary: The US government's pre-release model safety evaluation program — which tests for cybersecurity, biosecurity, infrastructure, and misuse vulnerabilities — expanded in May to include Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI alongside existing partners OpenAI and Anthropic.

headline: Broader macro: VC concentration in frontier infra and agents.
summary: Q1 2026 venture data shows $300B in startup investment globally, with $18.8B specifically into AI startups founded since 2025.

headline: Ollama 0.19 MLX backend (March 2026) now standard for Apple Silicon.
summary: Since March, Ollama has shipped its Apple Silicon inference backend on Apple's MLX framework rather than Metal + llama.cpp.

headline: MLX engine v1.8.1: parallel prediction for Qwen 3.5/3.6 and Gemma 4.
summary: An incremental update to Apple's MLX framework added parallel-prediction support for multi-image vision-capable models, specifically covering Qwen 3.5, Qwen 3.6, and Gemma 4 variants.

headline: Devstral Small 2505 is the current on-device agentic coding recommendation.
summary: Mistral's Devstral Small 2505 (24B parameters) fits on a Mac with 32GB unified memory or a single RTX 4090, making it the practical default for laptop-scale SWE-agent work.
